title: Judge Approves Major $1.5 Billion Settlement Between Anthropic and Authors
subtitle: A federal court has finalized a record-breaking agreement over the use of copyrighted books in AI training.

summary.en: A US federal judge has officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement between the AI company Anthropic and a group of authors. The lawsuit accused the company of using copyrighted books to train its artificial intelligence models without permission. Most authors involved in the case have already filed claims to receive their share of the money. While the judge supported the deal, she also decided to reduce the fees paid to the lawyers involved. Some authors remain unhappy with the payment amount and continue to pursue separate legal actions.
summary.zh: 一位美國聯邦法官已正式批准人工智慧公司 Anthropic 與一群作家之間十五億美元的和解協議。該訴訟指控該公司未經許可使用擁有著作權的書籍來訓練其人工智慧模型。大多數參與此案的作家已提交索賠申請以領取賠償金。儘管法官支持這項協議，她仍決定削減參與律師的費用。部分作家對賠償金額仍不滿意，並持續尋求個別的法律行動。

paragraphs.en: A significant legal battle in the world of technology has reached a major turning point. On Monday, a federal judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between the artificial intelligence company Anthropic and a large group of authors. This agreement marks the end of a massive class-action lawsuit, which is a legal case where a group of people with similar claims sue as one unit. According to Ars Technica, this is the largest copyright settlement ever reached.
paragraphs.zh: 科技界的一場重大法律戰已來到關鍵轉折點。週一，一位聯邦法官批准了人工智慧公司 Anthropic 與一大群作家之間高達十五億美元的和解協議。這項協議標誌著一場大規模集體訴訟的終結，所謂集體訴訟是指一群擁有相似主張的人聯合起來進行訴訟。根據科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導，這是史上金額最高的著作權和解案。

paragraphs.en: The case began in 2024 when authors Andrea Bartz, Charles Graeber, and Kirk Wallace Johnson sued Anthropic. They argued that the company used their copyrighted books to train its AI models without permission. As reported by The Verge, a previous judge had allowed a class-action lawsuit to move forward, describing the company’s actions as similar to the illegal downloading of millions of works. While Anthropic had previously won a partial victory regarding the use of books for AI training, the court still allowed the claims regarding the unauthorized copying of works to proceed.
paragraphs.zh: 此案始於二零二四年，當時作家安德烈亞·巴茨 (Andrea Bartz)、查爾斯·格雷伯 (Charles Graeber) 與柯克·華萊士·強森 (Kirk Wallace Johnson) 對 Anthropic 提起訴訟。他們主張該公司未經許可便使用他們擁有著作權的書籍來訓練其人工智慧模型。據科技新聞網站 The Verge 報導，先前曾有一位法官准許此集體訴訟繼續進行，並將該公司的行為形容為類似非法下載數百萬部作品。儘管 Anthropic 先前在關於使用書籍進行人工智慧訓練的爭議上贏得部分勝利，法院仍准許關於未經授權複製作品的指控繼續審理。

paragraphs.en: Under the terms of the approved settlement, authors will receive approximately $3,000 for each book that was allegedly used by the company. The Verge noted that this payment is considered by the legal team representing the authors to be the largest known copyright recovery in history. Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín, who oversaw the final approval, stated that the settlement provides meaningful relief to those affected. She also noted that the payment amount is four times higher than the minimum damages usually required by law.
paragraphs.zh: 根據已批准的和解條款，作家們針對該公司涉嫌使用的每一本書，將可獲得約三千美元的賠償。The Verge 指出，代表作家們的法律團隊認為這是史上已知金額最高的著作權賠償。負責監督最終批准程序的法官阿拉塞利·馬丁尼茲-奧爾金 (Araceli Martínez-Olguín) 表示，這項和解為受影響者提供了實質的補償。她也指出，賠償金額是法律通常要求最低損害賠償額的四倍。

paragraphs.en: Not everyone was happy with the deal, however. Earlier this year, some authors tried to block the settlement. They argued that the lawyers involved were taking too much money in fees and that the individual payouts for the authors were not high enough. Some of these writers hoped to opt out—a process of choosing not to participate in a legal settlement—so they could file their own separate lawsuits to seek more money. However, Judge Martínez-Olguín rejected these objections. She explained that the high level of participation in the program showed that most authors felt the deal was fair.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，並非所有人都對此協議感到滿意。今年稍早，一些作家試圖阻撓這項和解。他們主張參與此案的律師收取了過高的費用，且作家們獲得的個人賠償金額不足。部分作家希望選擇退出——即選擇不參與法律和解的程序——以便能提起各自獨立的訴訟來爭取更多賠償。不過，馬丁尼茲-奧爾金法官駁回了這些異議。她解釋說，該計畫的高參與度顯示大多數作家認為這項協議是公平的。

paragraphs.en: According to Ars Technica, about 95 percent of the people covered by the lawsuit received notifications about the deal, and roughly 91 percent of the authors and publishers have already filed claims to get their money. Only 350 people chose to opt out of the agreement. Anthropic’s general counsel, Aparna Sridhar, told The Verge that the company is pleased with the high level of participation and is looking forward to finishing this matter.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 Ars Technica 報導，約有百分之九十五的訴訟涵蓋對象收到了關於此協議的通知，且約有百分之九十一的作家與出版商已提交索賠申請以領取款項。僅有三百五十人選擇退出協議。Anthropic 的總法律顧問阿帕娜·斯里達爾 (Aparna Sridhar) 向 The Verge 表示，公司對於如此高的參與度感到欣慰，並期待能結束此案。

paragraphs.en: While the judge approved the total amount of the settlement, she did make changes to how the money is divided. She decided to lower the amount of money going to the lawyers. Originally, the legal teams asked for $300 million, which was 20 percent of the total settlement. This was later reduced to $187 million. Judge Martínez-Olguín ruled that even this lower amount was still too high. She cut the fees to less than 7 percent of the total fund, which equals about $101 million. She also reduced the special awards requested by the three lead authors who represented the class.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然法官批准了和解總金額，但她確實對款項的分配方式做了調整。她決定降低支付給律師的費用。最初，法律團隊要求三億美元，即總和解金的百分之二十。此後該金額降至一億八千七百萬美元。馬丁尼茲-奧爾金法官裁定，即使是這個較低的金額依然過高。她將律師費削減至總基金的百分之七以下，約合一億零一百萬美元。她同時也減少了代表集體訴訟的三位主要作家所要求的特別獎勵金。

paragraphs.en: Even with this settlement now approved, the legal challenges for Anthropic are not entirely over. The Verge reported that the company is still facing other copyright lawsuits from organizations like Chicken Soup for the Soul. These other groups continue to argue that the $3,000 payment per book is not enough to cover the value of their work. As the AI industry continues to grow, this case serves as a clear example of the ongoing tension between technology companies and the creators whose work helps build these powerful systems.
paragraphs.zh: 即便這項和解案現已獲准，Anthropic 面臨的法律挑戰仍未完全結束。The Verge 報導稱，該公司仍面臨來自「心靈雞湯」(Chicken Soup for the Soul) 等組織的其他著作權訴訟。這些團體持續主張，每本書三千美元的賠償不足以涵蓋其作品的價值。隨著人工智慧產業持續成長，此案清楚展現了科技公司與協助建立這些強大系統的創作者之間持續存在的緊張關係。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop something from happening or moving forward.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區、積木），這裡作動詞用。文中：Earlier this year, some authors tried to block the settlement.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To choose not to participate in an activity or agreement.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 選擇退出、不參加（某項協議或計畫）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是法律與商業常見的片語，指主動選擇不參與。文中：Some of these writers hoped to opt out—a process of choosing not to participate in a legal settlement—so they could file their own separate lawsuits to seek more money.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Financial or practical help given to people in need or suffering.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見意思為「鬆一口氣」，但在法律語境下指補償或救濟。文中：Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín, who oversaw the final approval, stated that the settlement provides meaningful relief to those affected.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge

quiz.question: What change did Judge Martínez-Olguín make regarding the financial distribution of the settlement?

options.A: She increased the payment per book to more than $3,000.
options.B: She reduced the legal fees from the original $300 million request.
options.C: She removed the special awards for the three lead authors entirely.
options.D: She mandated that the entire $1.5 billion be paid to authors immediately.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第六段，法官明確裁定律師團隊最初要求的 3 億美元費用過高，並最終將其削減至約 1.01 億美元。選項 A 錯誤，因為 3,000 美元是既定金額，法官未增加；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到法官減少了獎勵，但並非「完全移除」；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未提及支付的時間表或要求立即支付全部金額。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why did some authors want to 'opt out' of the settlement?

options.A: They believed that Anthropic had not actually used their books.
options.B: They were dissatisfied with the legal representation provided by the court.
options.C: They felt the compensation offered was insufficient compared to the value of their work.
options.D: They wanted to join the lawsuit filed by Chicken Soup for the Soul.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，部分作者試圖退出（opt out）是因為他們認為律師費用過高且「個人賠償金額不夠高」。這暗示他們認為透過獨立訴訟可能獲得更多賠償。選項 A 與事實相反，因為他們是為了求償；選項 B 雖提到律師費用問題，但並非對「法律代表」本身不滿；選項 D 文中並未提及這些作者想加入其他特定訴訟，僅提到他們想尋求更多賠償。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'tension' mean in the context of the relationship between AI companies and creators?

options.B: A state of mental anxiety or stress.
options.C: A strained relationship or conflict between opposing groups.
options.D: A formal agreement to work together.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在此語境下，tension 指的是科技公司與創作者之間因版權問題而產生的「緊張關係」或「衝突」。選項 A 是指物理上的拉力，不符合語境；選項 B 雖然有焦慮之意，但此處描述的是群體間的法律衝突而非個人心理狀態；選項 D 與 tension 的含義完全相反，因為 settlement 才是協議，而 tension 是指協議背後的矛盾。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: Anthropic has successfully proven that using copyrighted books for AI training is legal.
options.B: The legal battle between authors and Anthropic highlights the ongoing conflict over AI copyright.
options.C: Most authors are unhappy with the recent settlement and are planning to sue again.
options.D: Judge Martínez-Olguín is the most important figure in the history of copyright law.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。整篇文章圍繞著 Anthropic 的版權訴訟案，從和解金額、法官裁決到作者的反應，最終指出這是一個 AI 產業與創作者之間持續緊張關係的範例。選項 A 錯誤，因為公司並未獲勝，而是支付了鉅額和解金；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到 91% 的人已申請賠償，顯示多數人接受結果；選項 D 過度誇大，法官僅是此案的執行者，並非文章的核心主題。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
